title: SDALGCP2 — Design Notes
text: fast, statistically rich implementation spatially discrete approximation log-Gaussian Cox process. SDALGCP2 fits spatially discrete approximation log-Gaussian Cox process (SDA-LGCP) spatially aggregated disease counts, estimating parameters Monte Carlo Maximum Likelihood (MCML) predicting discrete continuous relative risk (method: Johnson, Diggle & Giorgi, 2019, Stat. Med. 38:4871–4887). document records (1) method , (2) current package slow statistically weak, (3) concrete plan SDALGCP2.

what: 1. The model and the approximation (so the speed work stays faithful)
title: SDALGCP2 — Design Notes
text: Disease counts Y_i regions A_i, = 1..N, modelled log-Gaussian Cox process aggregated area level: continuous areal integral ∫_{A_i} exp(S(x)) dx replaced discrete average candidate points placed inside region: Hence region-level random effect vector S* = (T_1,...,T_N) Gaussian N x N covariance Everything downstream (MCML, prediction) operates N x N object. candidate-point machinery exists build Sigma. split — expensive one-“build aggregated correlation” step, repeated cheap N x N linear algebra — key time spent. Estimation (Christensen 2004 MCML): 1. Pick anchor (phi0, sigma2_0), draw S* samples [S* | Y] Langevin–Hastings (MALA) sampler. 2. phi grid, maximise importance-sampling-corrected Monte Carlo log-likelihood (beta, sigma2) phi fixed. 3. Profile phi; report maximiser deviance-based CI. Spatio-temporal version: separable Sigma_space ⊗ Sigma_time, time correlation Matérn(kappa) range nu.
code: Y_i | S  ~  Poisson( m_i * exp( d_i' beta + T_i(S) ) ) S(.)      ~  zero-mean Gaussian process,  Cov{S(x),S(x')} = sigma2 * exp(-||x-x'||/phi) T_i(S) = sum_k w_ik S(x_ik)         (population-weighted, sum_k w_ik = 1)        = mean_k S(x_ik)             (unweighted) Sigma(phi, sigma2)_{ij} = sigma2 * sum_{k,l} w_ik w_jl exp(-||x_ik - x_jl|| / phi)

text: Pure-R double loop N(N+1)/2 region pairs; pair forms n_i x n_j distance matrix 3-D array exp(-outer(D, 1/phi)), reduces every phi. Cost ≈ O(N^2 * nbar^2 * n_phi) large constant factors R-level outer, array allocation, pdist. - PBC example (N = 545, ~tens points/region, 20 phi) wall-clock bottleneck allocates gigabytes transiently. - Fix: single RcppArmadillo kernel, OpenMP-parallel region pairs. Compute pairwise distance , accumulate weighted/mean exponential sum phi tight inner loop — 3-D array, per-phi R allocation. Expected 1–2 orders magnitude faster.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design Notes
text: Aggregated_poisson_log_MCML_ST materialises kronecker(inv.t.corr, inv.s.corr) — (N*T) x (N*T) dense matrix — log-det/products every likelihood, gradient Hessian call. Kronecker structure unnecessary: Fix: never form Kronecker product; reshape sample T x N matrix use identities. Turns (N*T)^3 work N^3 + T^3 plus cheap matrix multiplies. single biggest ST win. Also drop geoR::varcov.spatial (orphaned dependency) temporal Matérn — compute Matérn directly lines / C++.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design Notes
text: Goal (user request): let covariates supplied rasters z(x) enter model correctly, first averaging predictor polygon plugging average region-level GLM. Naive areal aggregation (\bar z_i)' \beta biased whenever exp link nonlinear z varies within region — ignores Jensen’s inequality. Principle: aggregate intensity scale, linear-predictor scale. aggregated-LGCP mean region x_ik candidate points already used build Sigma w_ik (population) weights. Factor covariate part : approximate inner sum log-normal aggregate (linear functional S plus variance correction): giving model keeps fast N-dimensional structure: right contribution - b_i(beta) log-sum-exp within-region covariate surface — (\bar z_i)'beta, reduces z constant A_i. - spatial aggregation weights c_ik(beta) tilted covariate intensity, high-covariate sub-areas contribute region effect — statistically coherent behaviour. - collapses basic region-constant model exactly covariates region-constant (c_ik = w_ik, b_i = z_i'beta, v_i absorbed intercept). Computational consequence & plan. Sigma offset now depend beta, clean “precompute correlation , profile cheaply” loop becomes iterated scheme: () hold beta fixed → rebuild tilted weights → assemble Sigma(beta) b(beta) via existing C++ kernel (already takes arbitrary weights), (ii) run MCML step, (iii) update beta, iterate convergence. cheaper first-order option keeps c_ik = w_ik (covariate enters offset b_i(beta) v_i), leaving Sigma beta-independent fully precomputable — still strictly better areal averaging. offered; default first-order offset model, fully-tilted version option. Implementation tracked task #5; design fixed .
code: E[Y_i | S] = m_i ∫_{A_i} exp(z(x)'beta + S(x)) rho(x) dx            ≈ m_i * sum_k w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta + S(x_ik))          (discretised) = m_i * exp(b_i(beta)) * sum_k c_ik(beta) exp(S(x_ik)),   b_i(beta)  = log sum_k w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta)        # log-sum-exp covariate offset   c_ik(beta) = w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta) / exp(b_i(beta)) # covariate-tilted weights, sum_k = 1 sum_k c_ik exp(S(x_ik)) ≈ exp( T_i(S;beta) + 0.5 v_i(beta) ),   T_i(S;beta) = sum_k c_ik(beta) S(x_ik)               # covariate-tilted spatial avg   v_i(beta)   = Var of that aggregation (from the point-level covariance) E[Y_i | S*] = m_i * exp( b_i(beta) + T_i(S*;beta) + 0.5 v_i(beta) ), S* ~ N(0, Sigma(phi, sigma2; beta)),  Sigma built with the tilted weights c_ik.

title: SDALGCP2 — Design Notes
text: Current behaviour. spatial scale phi discretised: evaluate aggregated correlation R(phi) grid, fit (beta, sigma2) , take profile maximum. grid robust (shows whole profile) wasteful — grid points far optimum — resolution caps precision phi-hat. Idea (user). Treat phi continuous parameter optimise directly inside MCML objective, alongside beta sigma2, instead scanning grid. MCML log-likelihood smooth phi, gradient-based optimiser needs R(phi) derivative dR/dphi arbitrary phi. Derivatives available closed form. aggregated exponential kernel, (general Matern: differentiate closed-form 3/2, 5/2 kernels similarly). MCML gradient w.r.t. log phi follows exactly existing spatio-temporal code, already temporal range nu: weighted importance weights reduced samples — vectorised machinery mcml_fit, plus one extra parameter. Plan. - New C++ kernel corr_and_grad_cpp(coords, weights, phi, kappa) returning R dR/dphi (reuses corr_aggregate inner loop). - mcml_fit(..., method = c("grid","direct")): "grid" stays default reference; "direct" optimises (beta, log sigma2, log phi) jointly nlminb/optim, recomputing R(phi)/R'(phi) per step (cheap now). - Standard errors phi come directly joint Hessian (loess/spline needed), profile plot can still produced demand. - Compare two: agreement phi-hat speed (direct need far fewer correlation builds 20-point grid). Keep options permanently — grid diagnostics/multimodal profiles, direct speed/precision. Tracked task #6; design fixed .
code: R_ij(phi)      = sum_{k,l} w_ik w_jl exp(-d_kl/phi) dR_ij/dphi     = sum_{k,l} w_ik w_jl exp(-d_kl/phi) * (d_kl / phi^2) d/dphi of the per-sample joint log-density   = -0.5 [ tr(R^{-1} R')  -  (S-mu)' R^{-1} R' R^{-1} (S-mu) / sigma2 ],   R' = dR/dphi

title: 1. Spatial disease mapping with SDALGCP2
text: observe disease counts YiY_i aggregated areal units AiA_i (=1,…,Ni=1,\dots,N) offset mim_i (expected count, e.g. population times baseline rate). SDALGCP2 fits spatially discrete approximation log-Gaussian Cox process: Yi∣S∼Poisson(mieηi),ηi=di⊤β+Si, Y_i \mid S \;\sim\; \mathrm{Poisson}\!\big(m_i\, e^{\eta_i}\big), \qquad \eta_i \;=\; d_i^\top\beta \;+\; S_i,  did_i area-level covariates S=(S1,…,SN)S=(S_1,\dots,S_N) Gaussian spatial random effect. SiS_i average area AiA_i continuous Gaussian process S(x)S(x) exponential covariance Cov{S(x),S(x′)}=σ2exp⁡(−‖x−x′‖/ϕ)\mathrm{Cov}\{S(x),S(x')\}=\sigma^2\exp(-\lVert x-x'\rVert/\phi); aggregating process areas gives N×NN\times N covariance built candidate points inside region (see Tutorial 4). parameters coefficients β\beta, spatial variance σ2\sigma^2 range ϕ\phi. Two quantities reported every area: Relative risk RRi=eηi=edi⊤β+Si\mathrm{RR}_i=e^{\eta_i}=e^{d_i^\top\beta+S_i} — full relative risk, including covariate effect (relative_risk column); Covariate-adjusted relative risk RRiadj=eSi\mathrm{RR}^{\mathrm{adj}}_i=e^{S_i} — residual spatial relative risk adjusting covariates (risk high/low beyond covariates explain?) — adjusted_rr column.

title: 1. Spatial disease mapping with SDALGCP2
text: sdalgcp() takes sf object whose columns hold response, covariates offset. package ships small simulated example, sdalgcp_data: 64 regions disease count (cases), covariate (x1), population offset (pop). generated true covariate effect 0.6 baseline log-rate -6, can check model recovers . crude SIR noisy -fits sparsely populated areas — exactly model smooths:
code: library(SDALGCP2) library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  data(sdalgcp_data) head(sdalgcp_data) #> Simple feature collection with 6 features and 4 fields #> Geometry type: POLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 0 ymin: 0 xmax: 15 ymax: 2.5 #> CRS:           NA #>   region cases          x1  pop                       geometry #> 1      1     2 -2.03331626 3840 POLYGON ((0 0, 2.5 0, 2.5 2... #> 2      2     3 -1.64601792 3985 POLYGON ((2.5 0, 5 0, 5 2.5... #> 3      3     1 -1.25871959 2236 POLYGON ((5 0, 7.5 0, 7.5 2... #> 4      4     0 -0.87142125  846 POLYGON ((7.5 0, 10 0, 10 2... #> 5      5     3 -0.48412292  874 POLYGON ((10 0, 12.5 0, 12.... #> 6      6    12 -0.09682458 2231 POLYGON ((12.5 0, 15 0, 15 ...  # crude standardised incidence ratio (SIR): observed / expected-at-overall-rate rate <- sum(sdalgcp_data$cases) / sum(sdalgcp_data$pop) sdalgcp_data$SIR <- sdalgcp_data$cases / (sdalgcp_data$pop * rate) plot(sdalgcp_data["SIR"], main = "Crude SIR (the data)") plot(sdalgcp_data["x1"],  main = "Covariate x1")

title: 1. Spatial disease mapping with SDALGCP2
text: One call. Candidate-point spacing, spatial range MCMC settings chosen automatically; reanchor re-simulates latent field optimum couple times reliable variance estimates. (set seed shorter MCMC run vignette quick reproducible; defaults longer.) covariate effect x1 estimated close true value 0.6, spatial range phi variance sigma^2 describe residual spatial structure, importance-sampling effective sample size reports reliable Monte Carlo likelihood .
code: set.seed(2024) fit <- sdalgcp(cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), data = sdalgcp_data,                control = sdalgcp_control(n_sim = 4000, burnin = 1000, thin = 5,                                          reanchor = 1)) summary(fit) #> Call: sdalgcp(formula = cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), data = sdalgcp_data,  #>     control = sdalgcp_control(n_sim = 4000, burnin = 1000, thin = 5,  #>         reanchor = 1)) #>  #> Coefficients: #>             Estimate Std.Err z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)   -6.226   0.143  -43.55  < 2e-16 *** #> x1             0.660   0.136    4.84  1.3e-06 *** #> sigma^2        0.876   0.378    2.32    0.021 *   #> phi            1.207   0.448    2.69    0.007 **  #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1 #>  #> Spatial scale phi: 1.20698 #> Log-likelihood: 0.574661 #> MC importance-sampling ESS: 102 / 600 (17%);  log-lik MC SE: 0.0902 #> Note: sigma^2 is the variance of the latent Gaussian process.

title: 6. Covariates measured on a different support
text: intensity-scale offset (Tutorial 2) needs covariate z(xik)z(x_{ik}) candidate points xikx_{ik}. zz observed elsewhere (1) model Gaussian process krige points, obtaining predictive mean μz(xik)\mu_z(x_{ik}) variance vz(xik)v_z(x_{ik}); (2) plug Berkson-corrected offset bi(β)=log⁡∑kwikexp⁡{μz(xik)⊤β+12β⊤Vz(xik)β}. b_i(\beta)=\log\sum_k w_{ik}\, \exp\!\Big\{\mu_z(x_{ik})^\top\beta+\tfrac12\,\beta^\top V_z(x_{ik})\,\beta\Big\}.  12β⊤Vzβ\tfrac12\beta^\top V_z\beta term propagates prediction uncertainty (plugging kriged mean alone attenuate β\beta); vz→0v_z\to0 recovers raster case. Derivation: math/confounding--misalignment.pdf.

title: 6. Covariates measured on a different support
text: covariate z observed 40 scattered monitors (sf points z column); outcome counts 8×8 lattice. Pass monitors covariates =; reg need z column: Internally covariate kriged monitors every candidate point:  covariate’s spatial model (range, nugget, variance) estimated maximum likelihood; SDALGCP2 fits outcome model Berkson-corrected offset.
code: library(SDALGCP2) library(sf)  set.seed(123) zf  <- function(xy) 1.5 * sin(xy[, 1] / 4) + 1.2 * cos(xy[, 2] / 5) + 0.5 * xy[, 1] / 20 reg <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(   st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))), n = c(8, 8))) N <- nrow(reg)  # simulate counts from the point-level intensity model (true beta_z = 0.8) pts <- sda_points(reg, delta = 1.0, method = 3); w <- lapply(pts, function(p) p$weight) b_true <- sapply(seq_len(N), function(i) {   Z <- cbind(1, zf(as.matrix(pts[[i]]$xy))); log(sum(w[[i]] * exp(as.numeric(Z %*% c(-6, 0.8))))) }) reg$pop   <- round(runif(N, 800, 5000)) reg$cases <- rpois(N, reg$pop * exp(b_true))  # the covariate, observed ONLY at 40 monitors mon <- st_as_sf(data.frame(x = runif(40, 0, 20), y = runif(40, 0, 20)), coords = c("x", "y")) mon$z <- zf(st_coordinates(mon)) fit <- sdalgcp(cases ~ z + offset(log(pop)), data = reg, covariates = list(z = mon)) fit$beta_opt["z"] #> 0.89   (true 0.8)

title: 6. Covariates measured on a different support
text: covariate reported averages polygons (e.g. coarser grid census tracts), supply polygons. SDALGCP2 detects geometry uses aggregated areal kriging, reusing C++ kernels build outcome correlation: cases covariate effect recovered data never observed outcome’s units — point monitors, polygons match outcome regions.

title: 2. Spatially continuous (raster) predictors
text: region’s expected count log-Gaussian Cox process 𝔼[Yi∣S]=mi∫Aiwi(x)exp⁡{z(x)⊤β+S(x)}dx≈mi∑kwikexp⁡{z(xik)⊤β+S(xik)}, \mathbb{E}[Y_i\mid S] \;=\; m_i\int_{A_i} w_i(x)\,\exp\{z(x)^\top\beta + S(x)\}\,dx \;\approx\; m_i\sum_{k} w_{ik}\,\exp\{z(x_{ik})^\top\beta + S(x_{ik})\},  sum candidate points xikx_{ik} inside AiA_i weights wikw_{ik}. covariate enters inside exponential. Factor : 𝔼[Yi∣S]≈miebi(β)∑kcikeS(xik),bi(β)=log⁡∑kwikexp⁡{z(xik)⊤β} \mathbb{E}[Y_i\mid S] \;\approx\; m_i\,e^{\,b_i(\beta)}\sum_k c_{ik}\,e^{S(x_{ik})}, \qquad \boxed{\,b_i(\beta)=\log\sum_k w_{ik}\,\exp\{z(x_{ik})^\top\beta\}\,}  region’s covariate contribution log-sum-exp bi(β)b_i(\beta), (z‾)⊤β(\bar z_i)^\top\beta z‾\bar z_i areal mean. Jensen’s inequality two differ whenever zz varies within region, regression z‾\bar z_i biased β\beta — badly sharp features (e.g. point sources) sit inside regions. Full derivation: math/raster-covariates-derivation.pdf.

title: 2. Spatially continuous (raster) predictors
text: covariate z sharp peaks (think pollution sources), supplied terra::SpatRaster, plus outcome counts 8×8 lattice. peaks sit inside regions, region mean loses : right panel — region mean — washed peaks .
code: library(SDALGCP2) library(sf) library(terra)  set.seed(3) # a raster covariate with 14 sharp Gaussian peaks r <- rast(xmin = 0, xmax = 20, ymin = 0, ymax = 20, resolution = 0.08) xy  <- xyFromCell(r, 1:ncell(r)) src <- matrix(runif(2 * 14, 1, 19), ncol = 2) values(r) <- rowSums(sapply(seq_len(nrow(src)), function(s)   3.2 * exp(-((xy[, 1] - src[s, 1])^2 + (xy[, 2] - src[s, 2])^2) / 0.5))) names(r) <- "z"  sh <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(   st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))), n = c(8, 8))) N <- nrow(sh)  # simulate counts from the point-level intensity model (true beta_z = 1) pts <- sda_points(sh, delta = 0.5, method = 3); w <- lapply(pts, function(p) p$weight) Z   <- lapply(pts, function(p) cbind(1, terra::extract(r, as.matrix(p$xy))[, "z"])) b_true <- sapply(seq_len(N), function(i) log(sum(w[[i]] * exp(as.numeric(Z[[i]] %*% c(-6, 1)))))) sh$pop   <- round(runif(N, 2000, 6000)) sh$cases <- rpois(N, sh$pop * exp(b_true)) sh$zbar  <- sapply(seq_len(N), function(i) sum(w[[i]] * Z[[i]][, 2]))  # areal mean

title: 5. Spatial confounding and restricted spatial regression
text: One remedy (Reich, Hodges & Zadnik 2006; Hughes & Haran 2013) constrains random effect orthogonal complement fixed-effect design. KK orthonormal basis null(D⊤)\mathrm{null}(D^\top), model becomes η=Dβ+Kα,α∼𝒩(0,σ2K⊤R(ϕ)K), \eta=D\beta+K\alpha,\qquad \alpha\sim\mathcal N\!\big(0,\sigma^2 K^\top R(\phi)K\big),  KαK\alpha reproduce column DD β\beta identified data, spatial smoothing. SDALGCP2 fits Laplace-approximate marginal likelihood (derivation: math/confounding--misalignment.pdf).
code: library(SDALGCP2) library(sf)  set.seed(2) regions <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(   st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))), n = c(8, 8))) N <- nrow(regions) pts <- sda_points(regions, delta = 1.2, method = 3) S   <- as.numeric(t(chol(0.7 * precompute_corr(pts, 4)$R[, , 1])) %*% rnorm(N)) regions$x1    <- as.numeric(scale(st_coordinates(st_centroid(regions))[, 1]))  # spatial gradient regions$pop   <- round(runif(N, 500, 3000)) regions$cases <- rpois(N, regions$pop * exp(-6 + 0.6 * regions$x1 + S))  # standard fit vs restricted spatial regression fit_std <- sdalgcp(cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), regions) fit_rsr <- sdalgcp(cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), regions,                    control = sdalgcp_control(confounding = "restricted"))

what: What RSR does — and what it does not
title: 5. Spatial confounding and restricted spatial regression
text: single-dataset estimate depends (random) alignment spatial field covariate, honest way see effect small simulation study — 24 data sets generated (true β=0.6\beta=0.6), fitting methods:  Two things stand : methods essentially unbiased average (0.60 vs 0.59) — spatial term genuine noise, hidden confounder, neither systematically attenuates β\beta; RSR reports much smaller standard error (0.074 vs 0.151). estimate identified purely within-covariate signal, looks precise. Caveat (important). small RSR standard error can anti-conservative: reflect across-data-set spread (≈0.27\approx0.27 ), treats spatial–covariate separation known. well-documented critique RSR (Hanks et al. 2015; Zimmerman & Ver Hoef 2021). Whether RSR helps depends causal role spatial term: just smooth noise, RSR gives clean, data-driven estimate; proxy unmeasured spatial confounder, unrestricted model may actually preferable.

title: 3. Spatio-temporal disease mapping
text: One row per region time, geometry repeated. four years 6×6 lattice:
code: library(SDALGCP2) library(sf)  set.seed(7) shp <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(   st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 18, ymax = 18))), n = c(6, 6))) N <- nrow(shp); times <- 2019:2022; T <- length(times)  # simulate separable space-time counts (true beta=0.5) pts <- sda_points(shp, delta = 1.4, method = 3) Rs  <- precompute_corr(pts, 3)$R[, , 1] Rt  <- SDALGCP2:::.temporal_corr(seq_len(T), 1.5, 0.5) L   <- t(chol(0.4 * kronecker(Rt, Rs))) x1  <- rnorm(N * T); pop <- round(runif(N * T, 1000, 5000)) y   <- rpois(N * T, pop * exp(-6 + 0.5 * x1 + as.numeric(L %*% rnorm(N * T))))  dat <- st_sf(   data.frame(cases = y, x1 = x1, pop = pop, year = rep(times, each = N)),   geometry = st_geometry(shp)[rep(seq_len(N), T)])

title: Fast spatial & spatio-temporal disease mapping
text: Easy: sdalgcp(formula, data) — feels like glm(); sensible defaults first fit needs tuning. Fast: aggregated correlation assembly, MALA sampler Monte Carlo likelihood run C++ (RcppArmadillo + OpenMP) — 8–10× faster end--end original, returning estimates (see Performance). Grid-free scale: spatial scale φ optimised continuously default (grid), proper standard error — see derivation PDF. Continuous covariates done right: rasters enter intensity scale (log-sum-exp), averaging predictors polygons — see raster PDF. Spatio-temporal without ever forming (N·T)² covariance. Honest uncertainty: re-anchored Monte Carlo likelihood, importance-sampling diagnostics, nugget term, model checking (residual Moran’s ).

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
code: SDALGCP2(   formula,   data,   my_shp,   delta,   phi = NULL,   method = 1L,   weighted = FALSE,   pop_shp = NULL,   kappa = 0.5,   par0 = NULL,   control.mcmc = NULL,   phi_method = c("grid", "direct"),   nugget = FALSE,   confounding = c("none", "restricted"),   reanchor = 0L,   rho = 0.55,   giveup = 1000L,   nthreads = 0L,   messages = FALSE )

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
text: formula model formula, e.g. cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)). data data frame model variables (one row per region). my_shp sf polygons (anything coercible via st_as_sf). delta candidate-point spacing. phi numeric vector spatial scale parameters profile; NULL, default grid sqrt(min area) extent/10. method point method: 1 = SSI, 2 = uniform, 3 = regular grid. weighted logical; population-weighted aggregation using pop_shp. pop_shp population-density SpatRaster (needed weighted). kappa Matern smoothness spatial kernel (0.5 default). par0 optional starting values c(beta, sigma2, phi). control.mcmc list control_mcmc. phi_method spatial scale estimated: "grid" (profile supplied phi grid, robust default) "direct" (optimise phi continuously inside MCML objective; exponential kernel ). See package vignette/PDF double-integral derivation. nugget logical; TRUE (requires phi_method = "direct") add unstructured region-level term, fitting covariance \(\sigma^2(R(\phi)+\nu )\) estimating relative nugget \(\nu=\tau^2/\sigma^2\) standard error. Absorbs overdispersion explained spatial structure. confounding "none" (default) "restricted" restricted spatial regression (constrains spatial random effect orthogonal fixed-effect design; fitted Laplace-approximate marginal likelihood). reanchor number re-anchoring passes: fitting, latent field re-simulated current optimum model refit, keeps importance weights near-uniform (raises MC effective sample size). 0 (default) fits ; 2-3 usually ample. rho, giveup point-generation controls. nthreads OpenMP threads correlation build. messages logical; print optimiser progress.

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2

title: Fit a spatial SDA-LGCP model — SDALGCP2
code: # \donttest{ library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE ## ---- simulate a lattice of regions and aggregated counts ---- set.seed(1) bound <- st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))) shp   <- st_sf(geometry = st_make_grid(bound, n = c(8, 8))) N     <- nrow(shp)  pts   <- sda_points(shp, delta = 1.2, method = 3)      # regular grid points phi_grid <- seq(1, 5, length.out = 8) corr  <- precompute_corr(pts, phi_grid) Sig   <- 0.5 * corr$R[, , which.min(abs(phi_grid - 2.5))] x1    <- as.numeric(scale(st_coordinates(st_centroid(shp))[, 1])) pop   <- round(runif(N, 500, 3000)) y     <- rpois(N, pop * exp(cbind(1, x1) %*% c(-6, 0.5) +                             as.numeric(t(chol(Sig)) %*% rnorm(N)))) dat   <- data.frame(y = y, x1 = x1, pop = pop)  ## ---- fit ---- ctrl <- control_mcmc(n.sim = 6000, burnin = 1500, thin = 6, h = 1.65 / N^(1/6)) fit  <- SDALGCP2(y ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), dat, shp, delta = 1.2,                  phi = phi_grid, method = 3, control.mcmc = ctrl) summary(fit) #> Call: SDALGCP2(formula = y ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), data = dat, my_shp = shp,  #>     delta = 1.2, phi = phi_grid, method = 3, control.mcmc = ctrl) #>  #> Coefficients: #>             Estimate Std.Err z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept)   -5.966   0.125  -47.62  < 2e-16 *** #> x1             0.824   0.107    7.72  1.2e-14 *** #> sigma^2        0.510   0.207    2.47    0.014 *   #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Spatial scale phi: 1.57143 #> Log-likelihood: 8.33903 #> MC importance-sampling ESS: 1 / 750 (0%);  log-lik MC SE: 0.876 #> Note: sigma^2 is the variance of the latent Gaussian process.  ## ---- predict ---- pred_d <- predict(fit, type = "discrete",   sampler = "mcmc",    control.mcmc = ctrl) pred_c <- predict(fit, type = "continuous", sampler = "laplace", cellsize = 1,                   control.mcmc = ctrl) # }

title: Fit a spatio-temporal SDA-LGCP model (Kronecker-free) — SDALGCP2_ST
code: # \donttest{ data(sdalgcp_data) shp <- sdalgcp_data ## build a 3-time panel (data frame, N*T rows ordered by time then region) times <- 1:3 dat <- do.call(rbind, lapply(times, function(t) {   d <- sf::st_drop_geometry(shp); d$time <- t   d$cases <- rpois(nrow(d), d$pop * exp(-6 + 0.6 * d$x1 + 0.1 * (t - 2)))   d })) fit <- SDALGCP2_ST(cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), dat, shp, times = times,                    delta = 1.5,                    control.mcmc = control_mcmc(n.sim = 2000, burnin = 500, thin = 5)) fit$phi_opt; fit$nu_opt #> [1] 2 #>            #> 0.8773136   ## restricted spatial regression against space-time confounding fit_c <- SDALGCP2_ST(cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), dat, shp, times = times,                      delta = 1.5, phi = c(2, 4, 6), confounding = "restricted") fit_c$beta_opt #> (Intercept)          x1  #>  -5.9945200   0.5845133   ## a spatially continuous (raster) covariate, aggregated on the intensity scale r <- terra::rast(terra::ext(0, 20, 0, 20), resolution = 0.5) terra::values(r) <- as.numeric(scale(terra::crds(r)[, 1])); names(r) <- "z" fit_r <- SDALGCP2_ST(cases ~ z + offset(log(pop)), dat, shp, times = times,                      delta = 1.5, phi = c(2, 4, 6), rasters = r, max_iter = 4,                      control.mcmc = control_mcmc(n.sim = 2000, burnin = 500, thin = 5)) fit_r$beta_opt #> (Intercept)           z  #>   -6.005052    0.490473  # }

title: Fit an SDA-LGCP with spatially continuous (raster) covariates — SDALGCP2_raster
text: Covariates supplied rasters enter model candidate-point level aggregated intensity (exp) scale via log-sum-exp offset \(b_i(\beta)=\log\sum_k w_{ik}\exp(z(x_{ik})^\top\beta)\) – statistically correct alternative averaging predictor polygon. Estimation Gauss-Newton fixed point reuses mcml_fit intensity-tilted effective design.

code: SDALGCP2_raster(   formula,   data,   my_shp,   delta,   rasters,   phi = NULL,   method = 3L,   weighted = FALSE,   pop_shp = NULL,   kappa = 0.5,   tilt_spatial = FALSE,   control.mcmc = NULL,   max_iter = 10L,   tol = 0.001,   messages = FALSE )

title: Fit an SDA-LGCP with spatially continuous (raster) covariates — SDALGCP2_raster
text: formula model formula; right-hand-side names must match raster layer names. response offset(log(pop)) come data. data data frame response offset (one row per region). my_shp sf polygons. delta candidate-point spacing. rasters terra::SpatRaster (object coercible terra::rast) whose layers spatially varying covariates named formula. phi spatial-scale grid (default chosen geometry). method, weighted, pop_shp point-generation controls (see sda_points). kappa Matern smoothness spatial correlation. tilt_spatial logical; FALSE (default) spatial correlation uses population weights precomputed (covariates enter log-sum-exp offset). TRUE, correlation \(R^c(\beta)\) rebuilt iteration intensity-tilted weights \(c_{ik}(\beta)\) log-normal aggregation correction \(\tfrac12\sigma^2(1-R^c_{ii})\) added – fully tilted model (accurate, costly). control.mcmc list control_mcmc. max_iter, tol outer Gauss-Newton iteration controls. messages logical; print progress.

text: formula model formula, e.g. cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)). data sf object polygons whose columns hold response, covariates offset (one row per region, per region-time spatio-temporal fits). time optional name time column data; given, spatio-temporal model fitted (data must one row per region time). rasters optional terra::SpatRaster spatially continuous covariates (layers named formula). covariates optional named list sf point layers giving covariates observed different support (e.g. monitors); kriged candidate points enters Berkson correction (see SDALGCP2_misaligned). popden optional population-density SpatRaster; supplied, region aggregation population-weighted. control sdalgcp_control list settings (smart defaults). verbose logical; print progress.

title: Fit a spatially discrete LGCP model for aggregated counts — sdalgcp
code: # \donttest{ library(sf) set.seed(1) grid <- st_make_grid(st_as_sfc(st_bbox(c(xmin = 0, ymin = 0, xmax = 20, ymax = 20))),                      n = c(8, 8)) regions <- st_sf(geometry = grid) regions$x1  <- as.numeric(scale(st_coordinates(st_centroid(regions))[, 1])) regions$pop <- round(runif(nrow(regions), 500, 3000)) regions$cases <- rpois(nrow(regions), regions$pop * exp(-6 + 0.5 * regions$x1))  fit <- sdalgcp(cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), data = regions)  # that's it summary(fit) #> Call: sdalgcp(formula = cases ~ x1 + offset(log(pop)), data = regions) #>  #> Coefficients: #>              Estimate   Std.Err z value Pr(>|z|)     #> (Intercept) -5.95e+00  6.45e-02  -92.23   <2e-16 *** #> x1           4.03e-01  3.33e-02   12.11   <2e-16 *** #> sigma^2      5.48e-01  2.14e-01    2.56    0.011 *   #> phi          2.38e-02  2.60e+04    0.00    1.000     #> --- #> Signif. codes:  0 ‘***’ 0.001 ‘**’ 0.01 ‘*’ 0.05 ‘.’ 0.1 ‘ ’ 1 #>  #> Spatial scale phi: 0.0237578 #> Log-likelihood: 0.22016 #> MC importance-sampling ESS: 5 / 1000 (1%);  log-lik MC SE: 0.425 #> Note: sigma^2 is the variance of the latent Gaussian process. rr <- predict(fit)          # an sf you can plot() directly plot(fit)                   # default relative-risk map  # }

text: delta candidate-point spacing. NULL (default) chosen automatically place roughly points_per_region points typical region. points_per_region target number candidate points per region used pick delta automatically. point_method candidate points laid : "regular" (deterministic grid, default), "uniform" "ssi". scale spatial scale \(\phi\) estimated: "continuous" (optimised directly, grid – default) "grid" (profiled phi). Spatio-temporal fits always profile \(\phi\) grid. phi optional \(\phi\) grid (used scale = "grid" spatio-temporal fits); chosen geometry NULL. kappa spatial Matern smoothness (0.5, 1.5 2.5). kappa_t temporal Matern smoothness (spatio-temporal fits). nugget logical; add unstructured region-level term (overdispersion). Requires scale = "continuous". confounding "none" (default) "restricted". "restricted", restricted spatial regression used: spatial random effect constrained orthogonal complement fixed-effect design absorb spatially structured covariate (avoids spatial confounding / attenuation beta). Spatial models . reanchor number re-anchoring passes (re-simulate latent field optimum refit) reliable variance estimates. Default 2. n_sim, burnin, thin MCMC length controls latent-field sampler. tilt_spatial logical; raster covariates, use fully covariate-tilted correlation (see SDALGCP2_raster). nthreads OpenMP threads correlation assembly (0 = default).

title: SDALGCP2 0.1.0
text: First public version. Raster, misaligned, restricted-regression covariates spatio-temporal model. SDALGCP2_ST() (sdalgcp(..., time =)) now accept rasters = (intensity-scale continuous covariates), covariates = (kriged covariates measured different support, Berkson correction), confounding = "restricted" (restricted spatial regression space-time confounding) — extensions previously available spatial fits. raster/misaligned fits use Gauss-Newton tilting loop around Kronecker-free space-time likelihood; restricted fit reduces exactly spatial restricted fit T = 1. Spatio-temporal prediction now returns long sf . predict() SDALGCP2_ST fit returns one-row-per-region-time sf (class "SDALGCP2_ST_pred") relative_risk/adjusted_rr columns spatial predictor, can mapped st_write()-en way; posterior draws kept attributes. Bundled datasets. sdalgcp_data — small simulated sf 64 regions (cases, x1, pop) used help-page examples intro vignette; liver — real example, incident primary biliary cirrhosis counts LSOA North East England (Johnson et al. 2019), realistic case studies. introductory vignette now runs live sdalgcp_data (precomputed figures), fully reproducible. Runnable examples exported functions, using sdalgcp_data. Prediction output now sf clear public-health column names. predict() returns sf (class "SDALGCP2_pred") can map st_write() directly, columns relative_risk/relative_risk_se (relative risk exp(d'beta + S)) adjusted_rr/adjusted_rr_se (covariate-adjusted relative risk exp(S)). previous RR/ARR names replaced everywhere (plot(), exceedance(), map_exceedance(), spatio-temporal predictor) — ARR dropped conventionally means absolute risk reduction epidemiology. Posterior draws retained object attributes exceedance probabilities still work either quantity. Kronecker-free spatio-temporal model (SDALGCP2_ST()): separable space-time SDA-LGCP counts regions several times. likelihood never forms (N*T)x(N*T) covariance, using tr(Rs^-1 M Rt^-1 M') quadratic form N log|Rt| + T log|Rs| log-determinant (verified vs brute-force Kronecker product). Spatial scale grid, temporal Matern range estimated continuously (analytic gradient verified vs numDeriv). geoR. Covariate-tilted raster model (SDALGCP2_raster(tilt_spatial = TRUE)): rebuilds correlation intensity-tilted weights log-normal aggregation correction. General Matern smoothness direct method: corr_and_grad_cpp now provides closed-form phi-derivatives Matern kappa {0.5, 1.5, 2.5}, continuous-phi (phi_method = "direct") estimation works three (exponential ). Derivatives verified finite differences. Nugget / overdispersion term (nugget = TRUE, phi_method = "direct"): fits covariance sigma2 (R(phi) + nu ) estimates relative nugget nu = tau2/sigma2 standard error, absorbing region-level overdispersion beyond spatial structure. Analytic gradient Hessian (including nugget cross terms) verified numerical differentiation. Re-anchored (iterated) MCML (reanchor =): re-simulates latent field current optimum refits, keeping importance weights near-uniform. 64-region example lifts effective sample size ~0% ~96% cuts log-likelihood MC standard error ~100x, correcting variance estimate. Importance-sampling diagnostics (mc_diagnostics(), shown summary()): effective sample size importance weights optimum Monte Carlo SE maximised log-likelihood; warns collapse. Spatially continuous (raster) covariates (SDALGCP2_raster()): covariates given rasters enter LGCP intensity candidate-point level aggregated intensity (exp) scale via log-sum-exp offset b_i(beta) = log sum_k w_ik exp(z(x_ik)'beta) – correct alternative averaging predictor polygons (biased log link). Fit Gauss-Newton fixed point reusing mcml_fit(). sharp-peak covariate, naive areal averaging biased +67% recovers truth ~6%. Continuous-phi (“direct”) estimation (phi_method = "direct"): optimises spatial scale phi continuously inside MCML objective instead profiling grid, using analytic first/second derivatives aggregated double integral (corr_and_grad_cpp). Gives genuine standard error phi joint Hessian avoids grid-boundary artefacts; phi_method = "grid" stays default. Gradient Hessian verified numerical differentiation. full derivation math/continuous-phi-derivation.pdf. Post-fit visualisation & diagnostics: relative-risk / uncertainty / exceedance maps, phi_profile(), coef_plot(), model_check() (residual Moran’s ), report(). C++ (RcppArmadillo + OpenMP) aggregated correlation assembly (precompute_corr). C++ Newton Laplace mode + adaptive MALA sampler (laplace_sampling); reproducible given mode seed. Vectorised, Cholesky-based Monte Carlo likelihood analytic gradient/Hessian (mcml_fit). One-call spatial fit SDALGCP2() (points -> correlation -> MCML). Candidate-point generation sda_points() (SSI / uniform / regular; sf + spatstat terra). Prediction predict.SDALGCP2() (discrete + continuous) MCMC -MCMC Laplace fast path; exceedance() probabilities. Vignette reproducible benchmark scripts using entirely simulated data.
